layout.footerBrand: AI photo and video studio. Fanvue and Telegram chat, team workflows, and billing in one workspace.
layout.billingSuccessBody: If the payment went through, your subscription or credits will update shortly (within a few minutes). Open your dashboard and check Plan & top-up if needed.
layout.billingCancelBody: You closed the checkout page or cancelled the attempt. Funds are usually not charged in this case.
layout.billingFailBody: If you were charged, save your receipt and contact support. Otherwise, retry payment from your dashboard.
layout.billingUnknownBody: Check your subscription status and credit balance in the dashboard.

hero.dek: Photo and video generation, Fanvue and Telegram chat, auto-translation, and team workflows — without juggling five separate tools.

community.title: Community and updates — in our channel.
community.dek: All community chat, news, and product discussion live in our Telegram channel. Feature requests, studio updates, and team replies — all in one place.

referral.title: Invite creators — earn on every payment.
referral.dek: While your referral stays with us, you earn {{ref.referrer_payment_percent}}% of every subscription payment — as credits at 1 cr. = {{ref.credit_unit_price_rub}} ₽. Card, credits, or renewal — no time limit. Invited users start with +{{ref.friend_referral_credits}} cr. via your link.
referral.note: Your personal link is in the dashboard under Plan & balance. Subscriptions can be paid from your credit balance.
referral.cardFriendHint: Signup bonus via your link (plus the standard {{ref.signup_base_credits}} cr. trial).
referral.cardReferrerHint: On every payment and renewal — again and again. Example per payment: {{ref.referrer_reward_example_rub}} ₽ → ~{{ref.referrer_reward_example_credits}} cr.
referral.cardPayHint: Any plan in the dashboard can be paid with credits from your balance at the same rate.
referral.ctaPrimary: Sign up and get your link

cards.title: Images
cards.desc: Model, reference, and prompt — GROK builds the scene. Nano Banana, Seedance, carousels, and refinements in history.

cards.title: Video
cards.desc: Reference clip, first frame, and brief — finished clips in your archive. Motion control and scenes tailored to your model.

cards.title: Chat & translation
cards.desc: One inbox for fans. Auto-translation across dozens of languages — your team writes in English, fans see their language.

items.title: Jumping between 5 services
items.text: Prompts, generation, translation, CRM, and team spreadsheets — context switching eats your day.

items.title: Every generation from scratch
items.text: Your model's look never sticks — back to "as before, but…" and random results again.

items.title: Chatters get mixed up
items.text: Multiple models — different personas and languages. Fans can tell when something feels off.

items.title: International fans
items.text: Robotic machine translation kills trust — and tips.

rows.dek: On Managed trial — bring your WaveSpeed key. On BYOK — always yours. Model profile and references stay in your dashboard.
Conversations in one window. Auto-translation for inbound and outbound — chatters keep each model's voice consistent.
Reference → model-ready prompt. Storyboards and motion briefs for video — no manual tab-hopping.

steps.title: Sign up and log in
steps.text: Create your account with email, sign in, and open the Connections section.

steps.title: Create a key at wavespeed.ai
steps.text: Register at wavespeed.ai, open API Keys, and copy your key.

steps.title: Add the key in Integrations
steps.text: Paste your WaveSpeed key in Connections → WaveSpeed and save.

steps.title: Build your model profile
steps.text: In Studio, create a model, upload 8–15 reference photos, and save.

steps.title: Generate with the right mode
steps.text: Images for shots and carousels, Video for motion clips, Chat for replies and translation.

steps.title: Connect Telegram/Fanvue
steps.text: After studio setup, connect channels in Connections to work from one inbox.

trial.title: {{betaCount}} users on the platform
trial.dek: {{demoGenerations}} free image generations on signup. On Pro, billing goes directly to WaveSpeed via your key. Standard uses platform credits for the studio.

cta.dek: Images, video, chats, and team — in ModelMate. {{demoGenerations}} free generations to start, no card required.

section.dekAfter: key (Pro) or platform credits (Standard). Annual billing — 25% off.

proExplainer.title: What is Pro?
proExplainer.p1Before: Pro — you connect an API key from
proExplainer.p1After: . Pay the provider directly at real generation rates — the platform does not charge credits for images and video. ModelMate charges for infrastructure, chat, and team features.
proExplainer.p2: Don't want to sign up for WaveSpeed? Choose Standard: studio credits are included in your subscription, with top-ups from 50 credits at current dashboard rates.

features.proKey: Your WaveSpeed key — no platform charges for generation

pricingPage.title: Pricing
pricingPage.intro: Six plans: Pro and Standard in three sizes (Solo, Pro, Studio). Pay in the dashboard via YooKassa. Start with {{demoGenerations}} free generations.
pricingPage.trialDek: {{demoGenerations}} free image generations on signup. For Pro, connect your WaveSpeed key in Integrations. Demo generations do not expire over time.

loginPage.title: Dashboard access
loginPage.intro: Log in or create a workspace — set up integrations and the studio after sign-in.
loginPage.legalPrefix: By continuing, you agree to the

faq.title: Frequently asked questions
faq.intro: Quick answers on using ModelMate: sign-in, channel setup, pricing, and the studio.

items.question: Where do I start after signing up?
paragraphs: 1) Log in to the dashboard. 2) Add your WaveSpeed key in Connections. 3) Create your model and upload references in Studio. 4) Use Images for shots, Video for clips, and Chat for translation/replies. 5) Then connect Telegram/Fanvue in Connections.

items.question: Why are both credits and WaveSpeed balance charged during trial?
paragraphs: This is intentional in trial: the system validates both billing paths — platform billing (ModelMate credits) and provider billing (WaveSpeed via your API key).
After you select a plan, billing behavior is fixed: BYOK charges generation on WaveSpeed, Managed uses platform credits according to your plan.

items.question: How do I sign up and log in?
paragraphs: On the login page, choose sign-up and enter an email and password at least eight characters long.
Go to login →

items.question: What is BYOK and why is it better?
paragraphs: BYOK means your own WaveSpeed API key: you pay the provider directly for generation with no platform markup on images and video.

items.question: How many plans are there and what's included?
paragraphs: Six subscriptions: BYOK and Managed × Solo / Pro / Studio. Annual billing saves 25%.
See the pricing page →

items.question: Referral program
paragraphs: Your link is in the dashboard → Plan & balance. A friend who signs up via your link gets +{{ref.friend_referral_credits}} credits (plus the {{ref.signup_base_credits}} cr. trial). You earn {{ref.referrer_payment_percent}}% of every payment they make — subscription, renewal, card, or credits — as credits at 1 cr. = {{ref.credit_unit_price_rub}} ₽. Example for one payment: {{ref.referrer_reward_example_rub}} ₽ → ~{{ref.referrer_reward_example_credits}} cr. to your balance; again on the next renewal.

BYOK or Managed subscriptions can be paid from your credit balance in the dashboard. On BYOK, WaveSpeed generations use your key — credits go toward ModelMate subscription and Managed studio, not WaveSpeed.

items.question: How is Managed different from BYOK?
paragraphs: Managed — studio generations spend credits from your balance; the platform may supply a WaveSpeed key after payment.
BYOK — you connect your own WaveSpeed key; credits are not spent on provider-side generation.

items.question: How do I pay for a subscription or credits?
paragraphs: The account owner purchases a subscription or credit top-up in the dashboard via YooKassa.

items.question: How does chat translation work?
paragraphs: Incoming messages can be shown in your language. You write in English — the fan receives text in theirs.

items.question: What do I need for the image studio?
paragraphs: Studio access, an active subscription (if the gate is enabled), and a WaveSpeed key in Integrations on Managed trial or always on BYOK.

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.intro: How ModelMate handles personal and operational data when you use the service. This text is informational; when deploying your own instance, update operator details and owner contacts.
privacy.meta: Last updated: {{year}}. Public ModelMate website version.

sections.title: 1. General
paragraphs: ModelMate (the "Service") helps teams manage conversations, integrations, and a content generation studio. This policy describes categories of data that may be processed when you use the Service and the general principles of that processing.
The data controller for a specific Service deployment is the person or organization that administers that instance and decides how user data is processed.

sections.title: 2. Data we may process
list: Account: email address, user identifier, password hash or other secure credential storage, profile settings, and access permissions within a workspace.

Content needed to provide the Service: message text and conversation metadata from connected channels (e.g. Telegram, Fanvue) when required to display threads, translate messages, and let operators reply.
Technical data: request logs, device and browser information (as needed for security and web app operation), session or authentication tokens.
Payment information: when paying via YooKassa, card payment details are processed by the payment provider under its rules; the Service may receive payment status and amount for billing purposes.
Integrations: API keys and secrets that a workspace owner enters for Telegram, Fanvue, translation providers, language models, and the studio — stored and used to run user-selected workflows.

sections.title: 3. Purposes of processing
paragraphs: Data is processed to:
list: authenticate users and enforce permissions within a workspace;
perform translations and other actions at the user's request;
maintain security, prevent abuse, and investigate incidents.

sections.title: 4. User content, AI, and liability
paragraphs: ModelMate is software and an interface layer for conversations, integrations, and the studio. Image, video, text, and other generation runs at the user's request using third-party models and APIs (including language models, image synthesis, and translation) that the user or instance administrator connects.

All content created, uploaded, imported, or published by users through the Service (including chats, prompts, generated media, and bot replies) is user content. Users and respective rights holders are responsible for that content and for complying with applicable law and platform rules (Telegram, Fanvue, etc.).

The operator of a ModelMate instance does not create that content on its own behalf, does not pre-moderate it for legality before publication, and is not liable for the substance, accuracy, legality, or consequences of user content or third-party AI output — to the fullest extent permitted by law. The Service is provided "as is"; output quality and safety from external models are not guaranteed.
If third-party rights, law, or integration rules are violated, the instance administrator may restrict access, remove materials, or disable accounts under the Terms of Service.

sections.title: 5. Sharing with third parties
paragraphs: Data may be shared with external providers only to the extent and for the purposes required by selected integrations and features (translation, LLM, image studio, messengers, etc.), and with the payment operator when you pay. Each provider's terms govern its processing.

sections.title: 6. Retention and security
paragraphs: Retention periods and technical measures are determined by the instance administrator. We recommend HTTPS, restricted account access, and regular rotation of integration secrets.

sections.title: 7. Cookies and local storage
paragraphs: The web app may use browser local storage or similar mechanisms to maintain login sessions and interface preferences. You can clear site data in your browser settings; you may need to log in again.

sections.title: 7. Your rights
paragraphs: Depending on applicable law, you may have the right to access, correct, delete, or restrict processing of your data, or withdraw consent. To exercise these rights, contact the administrator of the ModelMate instance you use.

sections.title: 9. Policy changes
paragraphs: The instance administrator may update this policy. The current version is published at this address; material changes should reasonably be communicated via the dashboard or email.

terms.title: Terms of Service
terms.intro: Terms for using the ModelMate Service. When deploying your own instance, replace contacts and legal details with current information; have legal counsel review if needed.
terms.meta: Last updated: {{year}}. Not a public offer unless explicitly stated by the operator.

sections.title: 1. Definitions
paragraphs: "Service" means the ModelMate software available through the web interface, including chat, integration settings, studio, and billing. "User" means an individual or legal entity using the Service. "Workspace owner" means the user on whose behalf a subscription is purchased and integrations are configured.

sections.title: 2. Acceptance and registration
paragraphs: Creating an account and/or logging in means you accept these terms as published at the time of use. You agree to provide accurate information and not share credentials with third parties except workspace members explicitly granted access by the owner.

sections.title: 3. Provision of the Service
paragraphs: Service features are provided "as is" within the capabilities of a given installation. The instance administrator may limit load, individual features, or geography for technical, legal, or operational reasons.

sections.title: 4. Plans, subscriptions, and credits
paragraphs: Subscription terms and pricing (including Managed and BYOK plans) and rules for purchasing studio credits are listed on the pricing page and in the dashboard. Payment may be processed via a payment operator (e.g. YooKassa). Access and credits are granted after successful payment confirmation.
See the pricing page →

sections.title: 5. Integrations, user content, and AI
paragraphs: Users configure bots, webhooks, and third-party keys independently and are fully responsible for the legality of their activity and all content they create or distribute.

The Service is an orchestration layer over third-party models and APIs; it does not guarantee the accuracy, safety, or fitness of generated materials. Messages and files are processed only as needed for selected features (display, translation, studio).

sections.title: 6. Prohibited conduct
paragraphs: You must not, among other things:
list: attempt unauthorized access to the Service, other workspaces, or user data;
use the Service in a way that creates excessive load or security risk;
violate third-party rights or applicable law.

sections.title: 7. Limitation of liability
paragraphs: To the maximum extent permitted by applicable law, the party providing the Service is not liable for indirect damages, lost profits, downtime of third-party services (including Telegram, Fanvue, AI and translation providers), user-generated content or third-party model output, or decisions made based on data obtained through the Service.

sections.title: 8. Intellectual property
paragraphs: Rights to the software, brand, and site materials belong to the rights holders. Rights to content created by users or imported from external channels remain with users or the respective rights holders.

sections.title: 9. Changes to terms
paragraphs: These terms may be updated; the new version is published on this page. Continued use after changes may constitute acceptance of the new version unless the administrator states otherwise.

sections.title: 10. Contact and governing law
paragraphs: Direct questions about these terms to the administrator of your ModelMate instance. Governing law and dispute resolution are determined by the operator of the specific installation.
